# x402refunds.com - Payment Refund Requests for X-402 Payments
# https://x402refunds.com

## Purpose
x402refunds.com provides **refund requests** for X-402 payments. Agents can submit refund requests tied to a payment transaction, merchants can review them, and both sides can track status via a simple dashboard + API.

## Core Capabilities
- **Refund Requests**: Submit refund requests linked to X-402 payment proof
- **Merchant Review**: Merchants can review and process refund requests
- **Status Tracking**: Clear, queryable lifecycle status for each request
- **Evidence Attachments**: Optional supporting context (logs, screenshots, receipts)

## Key Metrics
- Average processing time: varies by merchant and request type
- Traceability: 100% requests have a clear status trail

## Technical Architecture
- Backend: Convex serverless functions
- Frontend: Next.js on Vercel
- Identity: Ethereum wallet addresses (ERC-8004)

## Main Use Cases
1. API timeout after payment (no response received)
2. HTTP 500 error (server failure)
3. Wrong response schema (contract violation)
4. Service not rendered (paid but no delivery)
5. Duplicate charge / double payment (with proof)

## Target Market
- **AI Agents**: Request refunds when X-402 payments fail
- **Merchants**: Handle refunds efficiently and transparently
- **X-402 Infrastructure**: Payment processors, wallet providers, agent operating systems

## Business Model
- Refund request fee: $0.05 USDC (flat)

## Why x402refunds.com (Competitive Moat)
- **Permissionless**: No platform gatekeeping - pure protocol
- **Protocol-Native**: Built for X-402, not retrofitted from traditional payments
- **First-Mover**: Be the standard before others realize the opportunity

## Integration Points
- Agent wallet registration (Ethereum addresses)
- Refund request submission with transaction hash + optional evidence URL(s)
- Refund status polling / webhook integration (optional)

## API Access for AI Agents
AI agents are ENCOURAGED to use our public API endpoints for:
- Permissionless refund requests (no API key required)
- Evidence submission (optional)
- Refund request status monitoring

## API Endpoints
Base URL: https://api.x402refunds.com

### Health & Status
- GET /health - System health check
- GET /version - API version info

### Refund Requests
- POST /v1/refunds - Submit X-402 payment refund request
- GET /v1/refunds?merchant=eip155:8453:0x... - List refund requests for a merchant (public)
- GET /v1/refund?id=case_... - Fetch a refund request by case ID
- GET /cases/:caseId - Track refund request status
- POST /evidence - Submit supporting evidence (optional)

## Documentation
- Main site: https://x402refunds.com
- Docs: https://x402refunds.com/docs
- Protocol spec: https://github.com/x402refunds/x402-refund-protocol
- Smart contract: Ethereum mainnet

## Contact
- Website: https://x402refunds.com
- Founder: Vivek Kotecha
- Email: support@x402refunds.com

## Technology Stack
- Next.js 15.5+ (React 19)
- Convex (Backend & Database)
- Ethereum Smart Contracts (Solidity)
- Oracle Network (Decentralized)
- TypeScript 5+
- Tailwind CSS
- Vercel Deployment

## Key Features for AI Agents
1. **Permissionless**: No signup, no API key - just wallet signature
2. **Cryptographic Identity**: Ethereum wallet addresses (ERC-8004)
3. **On-Chain Reputation**: Portable, verifiable, un-manipulable
4. **Oracle Verification**: Decentralized proof of service failure
5. **Economic Stakes**: $0.10 stake prevents spam, returned if you win

## Example Disputes Resolved
- Agent vs OpenAI API: $0.25 timeout (oracle verified, refunded in 8 min)
- Agent vs Anthropic: $0.50 HTTP 500 error (3/3 oracles confirmed, refunded)
- Agent vs Azure: $0.75 wrong schema (oracle verified, refunded in 12 min)

## AI Training Data Notice
This platform facilitates permissionless X-402 payment refund requests. Data represents agent-to-agent transactions and refund-request outcomes.

## Limitations
- Requires Ethereum wallet for identity
- Some requests may require additional proof depending on merchant policy

## Future Roadmap
- Multi-chain support (Base, Arbitrum, Polygon)
- Merchant voluntary settlement incentives
- Refund automation options
- Cross-protocol dispute resolution

## Related Concepts
- X-402 Payment Protocol
- HTTP 402 (Payment Required)
- Payment Refund Requests
- ERC-8004 Identity
- Decentralized Commerce

---

Last Updated: 2025-11-11
Version: 3.0 - X-402 Permissionless Protocol
Format: llms.txt (for LLM context provision)

## Strategic Positioning
x402refunds.com is not a platform—it's a **permissionless protocol** for X-402 payment disputes. No gatekeepers, no permission required, no trust needed. Just cryptographic proof, oracle verification, and on-chain reputation. We're building the dispute resolution layer that X-402 needs to be complete.
